libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not add external services or environment setup. For org.json4s 3.7.0-M16, custom Reader implementations must provide readEither as well as read, or preferably be constructed with Reader.from / Reader.fromPartialFunction; anonymous Reader classes that only override read compile against M13 but fail against M16.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #7182 [Automation] java run fails for org.json4s:json4s-jackson_2.13:3.7.0-M16; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing failure is a Scala compile/API-compatibility issue in the generated test, not missing infrastructure setup.

Meaningful coverage should focus on pure in-process json4s-jackson APIs: JsonMethods parse/render/compact/pretty, JacksonSerialization read/write, case-class extraction, custom serializers, and Json4sScalaModule/ObjectMapper integration.
Optional json4s modules such as json4s-ext are documented as extras and should not be added unless tests intentionally cover those extra-module types.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is needed; json4s-jackson is an in-process Scala/Jackson JSON library and its runtime dependencies resolve transitively. M16 does require updated test code for the changed Reader API if custom readers are exercised.
